changes_made: Changed k8s_persistent_volume_backup_policy_defined to k8s_persistentvolume_backup_policy_defined for consistency with naming conventions
review_notes: The functions generally map well to the control CCCS CP-6, which focuses on backup and alternate storage. The naming conventions are mostly followed, but a minor change was made for consistency. The functions are technically accurate and align with Kubernetes capabilities for backup and encryption.
naming_improvements: Ensure 'persistentvolume' is used as a single word to match Kubernetes resource naming conventions
title: CCCS CP-6: Alternate Storage Site
step1_reasoning: The control CCCS CP-6 focuses on ensuring data is backed up and stored at an alternate site. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to ensuring etcd, which stores cluster state, is backed up and encrypted. Additionally, ensuring that Pods and Persistent Volumes have backup policies or annotations can align with the alternate storage site requirement by facilitating data recovery and redundancy.
